canto_title: The Vision of Cacciaguida and the Ancestral Legacy

scenes.summary: The poet reflects on how divine love and desire act with a balanced, rhythmic movement.
Dante contemplates how praying for transient things leads to a loss of true eternal love.
A luminous celestial event, resembling a shooting star, moves across the heavens.
The shade of Anchimates recognizes Dante and addresses him with divine grace.
Dante turns to Beatrice, overwhelmed by the radiant joy reflected in her eyes.
The spirit speaks so deeply that his words are initially difficult for a mortal to grasp.
As the spirit's intense emotion subsides, he offers a blessing to the Trinity.
Cacciaguida recognizes Dante as a student of great volumes and rewards his reading.
The ancestor explains that his joy is not greater than others in the Empyrean.
He explains that all souls see the truth instantly, and awaits Dante's purposeful voice.
Beatrice smiles at Dante, giving him the spiritual strength to begin speaking.
Dante notes the mathematical equality of affection and intellect in the celestial light.
Dante asks the 'living topaz' to provide him with sufficient ancestral wisdom.
Cacciaguida identifies himself as the root of Dante's very being.
Cacciaguida reveals his lineage and urges Dante to shorten his long ancestral labors through good deeds.
The spirit describes the modesty and simplicity of Florence in its early, pure days.
He recalls a time when homes were unpretentious and lacked excessive luxury.
He warns that the political power of Montemalo will inevitably fall like a climbing bird.
He contrasts the past with the present, where faces are painted and clothing is revealing.
He laments the loss of the certainty and domestic peace that once characterized the people.
He depicts the quiet scenes of mothers tending cradles and families sharing legends.
He mentions that figures of great virtue would have been marveled at in that era.
Cacciaguida recounts how the Virgin Mary brought him to this high place.
He traces his identity through his family connections and the origin of Dante's surname.
He describes his honorable military service under Emperor Conrad.
He recounts fighting against corrupt laws and finding eternal peace through martyrdom.
